title: Perpetuity: The Yotsuba Group (ヨツバグループ) offers a perpetuity of 12344 per...
summary: Calculate the effective annual rate or convert between APR and EAR under different compounding assumptions.
prompt:
The Yotsuba Group (ヨツバグループ) offers a perpetuity of $12,344 per year.
However, note that the first payment will be paid in 2 years. What is the present
value of the Yotsuba perpetuity if your discount rate is 12.1841% compounded
annually?
solution:
Step 1 Highlight Relevant Information
The Yotsuba Group (ヨツバグループ) offers a perpetuity of $12,344 per year.
However, note that the first payment will be paid in 2 years. What is the present
value of Yotsuba perpetuity if your discount rate is 12.1841% compounded
annually?
A few things to note:
The first highlighted bit suggests that we're dealing with a perpetuity (i.e., P
PMT/k); therefore, there's only one formula to use
Calculator studio solutions 3
The second highlighted bit suggests that our perpetuity's "PMT" remains to be
$12,344; and, that it occurs every year
The third highlighted bit informs us that our mission is to solve for the present
value of this perpetuity
The fourth highlighted bit (i.e., 12.1841% represents "QR"; however, this figure
must be compounded annually; therefore, this must also be our the Effective
Annual Rate EAR
Step 2 Build our Timeline
Note that our timeline seems to display that our present value isn't at PV0 just yet;
therefore, some further discounting will be needed in order to solve for the
ACTUAL present value of the Yotsuba perpetuity.
Step 3 Solving our Perpetuity
The formula for Perpetuities can be written as follows
PVn=PMTn+1kPV_{n}=\frac{PMT_{n+1}}{k}PVn=kPMTn+1
PMT2 $12,344
K 12.1841%
Such that, PV1 $101,312.38
However, this isn't our final answer. We must discount this amount to t0 in order to
find the PV of these cash flows
Step 4 Discounting Our Perpetuity to PV0
The formula for discounting cash flows can be written as follows
PV0PVn(1+k)nPV_{0\frac{PV_{n}}{(1+k)^{n}}PV0(1+k)nPVn
PV1 $101,312.38
K 12.1841%
N 1
Such that, PV0 $90,309.04
